title: WASM target — message passing from JavaScript
purpose: Enable JavaScript code to send messages to BEAM processes and receive messages back, bridging the actor model to the browser event model.

requirements.title: JS to BEAM message send
requirements.spec: JavaScript can send a message to a BEAM process by PID.
acceptance: send_message(pid, js_value) converts the JS value to a Term and enqueues in the process mailbox
Process receives the message via normal selective receive
Test: JS sends a message, BEAM process receives and processes it

requirements.title: BEAM to JS callback
requirements.spec: BEAM processes can invoke JavaScript callbacks via a registered NIF.
acceptance: js_callback NIF calls a registered JavaScript function with converted arguments
Return value is converted back to a Term
Async callbacks return Promises (bridged via B-145 R2)
Test: BEAM process calls JS function, receives result

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement BEAM-to-JS streaming — single message/callback only
SHALL NOT implement JS object proxying as BEAM terms
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
